notes_md:
**leo** = to climb, to go up something using your hands and feet. It’s used for physical climbing like mountains, stairs, trees, walls.

You use **leo + object** for the thing you climb. Add **lên** to emphasize upward movement.


It also appears in **giá leo thang** = prices go up quickly (literally “climb stairs”).  
Close synonym: **trèo** (very similar, often for trees); **leo** is a bit more general.

translation.en: I want to climb this mountain.

exampleSentences: Em muốn leo lên đỉnh núi với bạn vào cuối tuần này.
Anh leo cầu thang nhanh quá, em không theo kịp.
Chị thường leo cây khi còn nhỏ ở quê.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I want to climb the top of the mountain with you this weekend.
You climb the stairs so fast, I can't keep up.
She often climbed trees when she was little in the countryside.

definitions.es: Ascender o subir, especialmente en referencia a montañas o alturas.
definitions.en: To ascend or go up, especially in reference to mountains or heights.
definitions.vi: Leo là hành động đi lên, đặc biệt là liên quan đến núi hoặc độ cao.

example.vi: Tôi thích leo núi vào cuối tuần.
example.es: Me gusta escalar montañas los fines de semana.
example.en: I like to climb mountains on the weekend.

Close synonym: trèo (very similar, often for trees); leo is a bit more general.
